title: agentgovernance/v1 (AIP-7)
description: Validates the four AIP-7 doctypes: signature (approval event), audit-event (append-only log line), policy (declarative autonomy rule), and governance.workspace/v1 (workspace manifest, root or view). Branch on the `doctype` (or for the workspace, the `schema`) field.

schemaTag.description: Doctype family. Constant across all three doctypes.

isoDateTime.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp with millisecond precision in UTC.

principalId.description: Stable identifier of a principal (user id, agent id, service account). Slug or DID, never a numeric DB id.

hexHash.description: Lowercase hex hash digest, or the literal 'GENESIS' for the first entry's prevHash.

value.description: Base64url-encoded signature bytes over the canonical JSON of the signed payload.

publicKeyRef.description: Reference resolvable in the workspace keyring (DID, key id, JWK thumbprint).

artifact.description: Slug or workspace-relative path of the artifact being approved.

reason.description: Required when decision is 'reject'. Recommended for 'approve' in multi-signer flows.

seq.description: Monotonic sequence number per chain. Starts at 0; gaps are spec violations.

action.description: Dot-separated action class: tool.call, workflow.start, workspace.write, mutation.commit, audit.note, etc.

items.description: Workspace-relative path to a signature.json file.

hash.description: Hash of the canonical JSON of every other field. Recomputed on read.

action.description: Action class to match. May contain * as a wildcard segment.

subject.description: Optional. Literal id, glob, or workspace path. Wildcard matches everything.

autonomy.description: Maximum autonomy level at which this policy stays permissive. Above that, the policy escalates.

workspace.title: governance.workspace/v1 (manifest, root or view)
workspace.description: Workspace manifest doctype for AIP-7. Used both as the root manifest of a governance scope (no `extends`) and as a per-consumer view (with `extends`). The same schema validates both modes; the host distinguishes by checking whether `extends` is set. One-way switches (audit.appendOnly, signing.required) are enforced by the host at merge time and cannot be relaxed by a descendant view.

schema.description: Discriminator for the workspace manifest doctype. Note: this differs from the family-level $schema tag used by signature/audit-event/policy because the workspace doctype follows the AIP-10/AIP-13 manifest convention.

name.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for the workspace or view.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of purpose: what this workspace (or view) governs and who uses it.

version.description: Semantic version of the WORKSPACE shape. Bump on autonomy / policies / approvers / signing / audit changes.

extends.description: OPTIONAL — relative path to a parent GOVERNANCE.md. Presence of this field makes the manifest a VIEW; absence makes it a WORKSPACE ROOT. Recursive composition allowed; maximum chain depth is 8. Cycles and missing parents degrade to warnings; ONE-WAY SWITCHES (audit.appendOnly, signing.required) cannot be relaxed and produce hard refusals.

items.description: Either a ws:// ref to an AIP-9 operator / AIP-6 company / AIP-3 skill, or a relative path to a consumer workspace folder.

appliesTo.description: OPTIONAL — list of consumers this VIEW adapts the workspace for. Hosts MUST refuse the view if any binding does not resolve. Not inherited; views declare their own scope.

level.description: Autonomy ceiling. 0 = read-only, 1 = suggest, 2 = bounded auto, 3 = irreversible auto. Maps to the AIP-7 0-4 ladder; the workspace manifest declares the CEILING for this scope, individual policies MAY further restrict.

defaultApproval.description: Default approval class when no policy matches. 'auto' = no approval; 'always' = always require approval; 'on-mutate' = require approval on mutation; 'policy:<slug>' = delegate to a named policy.

approvalEscalation.description: Fallback route when defaultApproval can't resolve a quorum. 'from' and 'to' name approval classes.

autonomy.description: Autonomy posture for the scope. Each leaf field overrides independently across the chain.

algo.description: Default signing algorithm. Hosts MUST support ed25519; others are OPTIONAL.

keyring.description: Workspace-relative path to a public-key bundle (e.g. .well-known/governance-keys.json). The host loads keys from this path to verify signatures.

required.description: ONE-WAY SWITCH. When true, every audit event MUST carry a verifiable signature. Once a parent locks this to true, no descendant view may downgrade to false; hosts MUST refuse the merge with `governance_signing_downgrade`.

signing.description: Signing posture. Composes leaf-by-leaf across the chain. The `required` field is a one-way switch: parent strict → child stricter or equal.

hashAlgo.description: Hash algorithm for the audit chain. Default sha256.

appendOnly.description: ONE-WAY SWITCH. When true, the audit log is locked append-only — no in-place edits, no truncation, no deletion. Once a parent locks this to true, no descendant view may downgrade to false; hosts MUST refuse the merge with `governance_append_only_relaxation`. This is AIP-7's defining safety property.

storage.description: OPTIONAL — vendor-neutral URI of the audit store (e.g. file://, s3://, https://). Informational; the canonical artifact is still audit-log.jsonl.

headPointerSign.description: OPTIONAL — when true, the host periodically publishes a signed head-pointer attestation that external verifiers can pin against.

audit.description: Audit posture. Each leaf field overrides independently EXCEPT for `appendOnly`, which is a one-way switch (parent true → child cannot set false).

id.description: Stable kebab-case policy registry id. Merge key when composing with the extends parent.

ref.description: Workspace-relative path to a POLICY.md file. Policies are ALWAYS file-referenced, never inlined; this keeps the policy registry editable through the same governance gate as any other artifact.

appliesTo.description: AIP-7 action class to bind this policy to (e.g. 'workspace.write', 'tool.call', 'mutates') or '*' for all actions.

severity.description: Severity when this policy is violated. 'error' = block; 'warn' = surface; 'info' = log only.

params.description: Policy-instance parameters that the referenced POLICY.md interprets.

policies.description: Registry of policies that apply in this scope. Merge-by-id against the extends parent: a child entry with the same id replaces the parent's; new ids are appended.

id.description: Stable kebab-case approver id. Merge key when composing with the extends parent.

role.description: Human-readable role name OR an AIP-9 ws://operators/<slug> ref. Resolved at evaluation time.

canApprove.description: Approval classes this approver is authorized for.

quorum.description: Quorum requirement. '1' = single-signer; 'n-of-m' = quorum among a group of m.

approvers.description: Pre-declared approvers. Merge-by-id against the extends parent: a child entry with the same id replaces the parent's; new ids are appended.

executor.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-9 operator the host activates to run governance flows for this scope (apply policies, collect signatures, append audit entries).

escalateTo.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-9 operator to escalate to when autonomy.approvalEscalation fires. Distinct from `executor`: the executor runs governance, the escalation operator decides outcomes when the executor cannot.

work.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-13 WORK.md binding. Audit events emitted by this scope MAY also produce work-item updates in the bound work workspace.

knowledge.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-10 KNOWLEDGE.md binding. Schema or source mutations in the bound wiki flow through this workspace's approval gate.

defaultDashboard.description: OPTIONAL — slug of the dashboard a UI uses as the workspace landing surface.

showRetentionWarnings.description: OPTIONAL — when true, UIs surface a banner when retention windows are about to expire.

display.description: Display hints for UIs that render the workspace. Runtime-agnostic.

metadata.description: Vendor-specific extensions, namespaced under <vendor>. Deep-merged across the extends chain.

allOf.description: A view (any manifest with appliesTo set) MUST extend a parent. A workspace-root manifest has neither field.
